reasoning: The Lean statement correctly captures "odd prime p" via `Nat.Prime p` and `Odd p`. The sum `∑ i ∈ Finset.range (p-1), (1/(i+1) : ℚ)` correctly enumerates 1/1 + 1/2 + ... + 1/(p-1) in ℚ. The expression `(a/b : ℚ)` with `a b : ℤ` elaborates as casting to ℚ and dividing in ℚ. The universal quantification over all integer pairs (a,b) representing the sum is equivalent to the informal "specific representation" reading: if H_{p-1} = n/d in lowest terms with p∣n, then any a/b = n/d forces a to be a multiple of n (hence of p); the degenerate b=0 case gives 0/0 = 0 ≠ H_{p-1} so the implication holds vacuously. The conclusion `↑p ∣ a` matches exactly.
